faults.location: Section 3.4.1 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Taxi
faults.description: No signal in 3.4.1 to notify the taxi that its submitted order was rejected (e.g. no available cars). Variant 4b requires the central to reject the order and prompt a new submission, but no rejection signal exists for the Central‚ÜíTaxi direction.

faults.location: Section 3.1 ‚Äì Order_struct
faults.description: Order_struct has no Priority field. UC 1.2 Task 2 explicitly states that orders can be prioritized (some customers are more important than others), but no priority attribute is defined in the data structure.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Operator
faults.description: No signal in 3.3.2 conveys an estimated wait time to the operator. Variant 3b requires the system to present an estimate of how long it takes for a car to be available when an order is very close in time.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 ‚Äì Signals from User to Taxi module
faults.description: LoginSignal is defined with no parameters. UC 1.4 Task 2‚Äì3 requires that driver identification (from the swiped card) and position information be sent to the central upon login; without parameters, neither can be conveyed.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 ‚Äì Signals from Taxi to Central
faults.description: LoggedIn(integer) carries only the taxi number. UC 1.4 Task 3 requires that driver information be sent to the central together with the position at login, but no driver identification parameter is included in the signal.

faults.location: Section 3.4.1 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Taxi
faults.description: No LoginFailed signal is defined in 3.4.1. Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid driver card and keep the car in Offline state; without a Central‚ÜíTaxi rejection signal, the taxi cannot be notified of failed authentication.

faults.location: Section 3.4.1 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Taxi
faults.description: No ZoneInfo signal is defined in 3.4.1 (Central‚ÜíTaxi). UC 1.4 Task 6 requires the central to start sending overview information on all zones to the car after successful login; ZoneInfo exists only in 3.3.2 (Central‚ÜíOperator) and 3.2.2 (Taxi‚ÜíDriver), not in the Central‚ÜíTaxi interface.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 ‚Äì Signals from Taxi to Central
faults.description: Position(integer, integer) carries only taxi number and zone number. UC 1.6 Task 8 requires the driver to send both the arrival zone AND the estimated arrival time to the central; there is no time parameter in the signal.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 ‚Äì Signals from Taxi to Central
faults.description: No alarm activation signal is defined in 3.4.2. UC 1.7 Task 1 and UC 1.8 Task 1‚Äì2 require the taxi to immediately notify the central when the driver triggers the alarm button; no dedicated alarm signal exists in the Taxi‚ÜíCentral interface.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 ‚Äì Signals from User to Taxi module
faults.description: No alarm activation signal is defined in 3.2.1. UC 1.8 Task 1 requires the driver to trigger the alarm; only Reset_Alarm exists in 3.2.1, which is a reset action, not an activation.

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 ‚Äì Signals from Taxi to Central
faults.description: Position(integer, integer) sends only zone number during normal operation. UC 1.8 Task 3 explicitly requires the taxi to send exact coordinates to the central every 30 seconds while the alarm is active; a zone number does not satisfy this requirement.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 ‚Äì Signals from User to Taxi module
faults.description: Reset_Alarm(integer) is defined in 3.2.1, allowing the driver to send an alarm reset. UC 1.8 Task 4 states the alarm can only be reset by the operator, not the driver; this signal enables a driver-side reset that contradicts the use case.

faults.location: Section 3.4.1 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Taxi
faults.description: No ZoneInfo (or equivalent overview) signal is defined in 3.4.1. UC 1.9 Task 2 requires overview information to be sent from the central to the taxi terminal; this signal path is missing in the Central‚ÜíTaxi API (same structural gap as noted for UC 1.4 Task 6).

faults.location: Section 3.4.2 ‚Äì Signals from Taxi to Central
faults.description: No text message acknowledgment signal is defined in 3.4.2. UC 1.11 Task 4 requires each receiving car to automatically acknowledge text message reception to the central; the existing Ack(integer, integer) signal is for order acceptance, not text messages.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Operator
faults.description: No text message delivery confirmation signal is defined in 3.3.2. UC 1.11 Task 5 and variants 4b/4c require the system to confirm delivery (or non-delivery) of text messages back to the operator; no such signal exists in the Central‚ÜíOperator interface.

faults.use_case: UC 1.12 ‚Äì Central: Log in to central system
faults.location: Section 3.3.1 ‚Äì Signals from Operator to Central
faults.description: No operator login signal (carrying user identification and password) is defined in 3.3.1. UC 1.12 Task 1‚Äì2 requires operators to submit credentials to the system for verification, but no such signal exists in the Operator‚ÜíCentral interface.

faults.use_case: UC 1.12 ‚Äì Central: Log in to central system
faults.location: Section 3.3.2 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Operator
faults.description: No operator login confirmation (success or failure) signal is defined in 3.3.2. UC 1.12 Tasks 2‚Äì3 and variant 2b require the system to notify the operator of login success or failure, but no such signal exists.

faults.location: Section 3.3.1 ‚Äì Signals from Operator to Central
faults.description: Taxi(integer, integer, integer) is defined in 3.3.1 as a signal from operator to central, but its description reads 'this is the response' ‚Äî it is a Central‚ÜíOperator response signal incorrectly placed in the Operator‚ÜíCentral section.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 ‚Äì Signals from User to Taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.2.1 for the driver to request car or driver properties. UC 1.16 Task 1 requires the driver to initiate the property view on the terminal, but no corresponding request signal exists in the User‚ÜíTaxi interface.

faults.location: Section 3.4.1 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Taxi; Section 3.2.2 ‚Äì Signals from Taxi to Driver
faults.description: No signal for sending car or driver properties to the taxi is defined in 3.4.1 or 3.2.2. UC 1.16 Task 2 requires the central to send this information to the car terminal, but the entire Central‚ÜíTaxi‚ÜíDriver properties delivery path is missing.

faults.location: Section 3.3.1 ‚Äì Signals from Operator to Central
faults.description: No signal for an operator to accept (take) an incoming voice call from a taxi is defined in 3.3.1. UC 1.17 Task 1 requires a free operator to take the incoming communication request, but only outbound Start_Voice and Terminate_Voice exist for voice in the Operator‚ÜíCentral direction.

faults.location: Section 3.3.1 ‚Äì Signals from Operator to Central
faults.description: No signal for updating driver properties is defined in 3.3.1. UC 1.18 Task 3 requires the operator to submit updated driver properties to the system, but no such signal exists in the Operator‚ÜíCentral interface.

faults.location: Section 3.3.2 ‚Äì Signals from Central to Operator
faults.description: No acknowledgment signal for driver property updates is defined in 3.3.2. UC 1.18 Task 4 requires the system to acknowledge property changes to the operator, but no corresponding signal exists in the Central‚ÜíOperator interface.

faults.location: Section 3.2.1 ‚Äì Signals from User to Taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in 3.2.1 for the driver to acknowledge reading a text message. UC 1.19 Task 3 requires the message to be marked as read when the driver presses the button to read it, but no corresponding User‚ÜíTaxi signal exists.

faults.location: Section 4.1 ‚Äì MSC Order, Step 1
faults.description: MSC 4.1 Step 1 uses signal name Order(New_Order) for the Operator‚ÜíCentral direction, but the signal defined in 3.3.1 for this direction is OrderOper(Order_struct, integer). The MSC references a non-existent signal name.

faults.location: Section 4.1 ‚Äì MSC Order, after Step 8
faults.description: MSC 4.1 shows no Confirm signal from Central‚ÜíTaxi after the Ack is received (after Step 8). UC 1.5 Task 4 and UC 1.10 Task 4 require the central to confirm the order acceptance back to the taxi; this step is absent from the MSC.

faults.location: Section 4.2 ‚Äì MSC Voice, Steps 2‚Äì3
faults.description: MSC 4.2 Steps 2‚Äì3 pass Start_Voice(Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr) from Central‚ÜíComm_link‚ÜíTaxi, but 3.4.1 defines the Central‚ÜíTaxi Start_Voice parameters as (taxi_number, operator_number). The parameter order in the MSC is inverted relative to the signal definition.

faults.location: Section 4.2 ‚Äì MSC Voice, Step 5
faults.description: MSC 4.2 Step 5 shows Driver‚ÜíTaxi: Confirm_Voice(Taxi_Nbr), but no Confirm_Voice signal is defined in 3.2.1 (Signals from User to Taxi module). The signal Confirm_Voice exists in 3.2.2 (sent to the driver), not from the driver.

faults.location: Section 4.2 ‚Äì MSC Voice, Step 15
faults.description: MSC 4.2 Step 15 shows Driver‚ÜíTaxi: Start_Voice(Taxi_Nbr) after the voice channel is already established (post-Confirm_Voice at Step 5‚Äì8). At this point the driver should use Send_Voice(Taxi_Nbr) per 3.2.1 to transmit speech; Start_Voice is an initiation signal and is incorrect here.
